data.title: Investors Flock to the Global X Defense Tech ETF (SHLD) after the U.S. Successfully Captured Maduro
data.description:
Investors are flocking to the Global X Defense Tech ETF (SHLD) as the world becomes more uncertain in 2026. This fund doesn't just own companies that make tanks and planes. It focuses on "future tech" like AI, drones, and computer security. Because several big global events happened at the same time, this ETF has become a top choice for people who think countries will keep spending more on their militaries.

As seen in the chart below, the fund has experienced a powerful breakout rally. Since the start of 2026, SHLD has jumped from $69 to over $76, marking one of its strongest two-week runs since it launched.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stocks Face 'Near-Term Underperformance' on Fed Independence Risks, Says JPMorgan
data.description:
The trump">Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell has sparked concerns that the central bank's independence is at risk. That could be problematic for U.S. stocks, according to JPMorgan (JPM).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The risk around Fed independence is likely to push the US toward near-term underperformance," said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence.

DOJ Probe Poses Risk to Fed Credibility, Stocks

The Fed's primary goal is to maximize employment and keep inflation contained. However, bowing to political pressure could undermine its credibility and ability to manage the economy effectively.

Tyler said that the investigation creates an "overhang" for U.S. stocks, despite a positive macroeconomic backdrop and strong corporate fundamentals. He added that a buying opportunity could emerge if the Trump administration eases its challenge to Fed independence.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Partners with Nvidia on New AI Drug Laboratory
data.description:
U.S. pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly (LLY) is partnering with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) on a new artificial intelligence (AI) research lab to be based in San Francisco, California.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Eli Lilly and Nvidia say they will spend a combined $1 billion to build the AI research lab over the next five years. The laboratory will reportedly use Nvidia's newest Vera Rubin AI chips, the companies said in a joint news release.

The AI lab announcement was made at the JPMorgan(JPM) Healthcare Conference that's being held this week in San Francisco. The laboratory is being developed months after Eli Lilly announced plans to build a supercomputer that will use more than 1,000 of Nvidia's Blackwell AI microchips and processors.

AI's Impact on Healthcare

Eli Lilly is among several pharma companies that is experimenting with and harnessing the power of sophisticated AI models to discover new medical treatments. Analysts say they healthcare is one of the sectors most likely to be disrupted by AI through disease diagnoses and treatment.

For its part, Nvidia is looking to supply its AI processors, models and software to drug makers such as Eli Lilly so that they can build drug development platforms using Nvidia's microchips and hardware. It is not clear if Nvidia's investment in the new AI drug laboratory will flow directly to Eli Lilly and be used to buy Nvidia microchips.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,192.17 implies 11.52% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Paramount Skydance (PSKY) Sues Warner Bros. Discovery in Takeover Fight
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has stepped up its fight to take over Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) by suing the company for more information about its $82.7 billion deal with Netflix (NFLX). The company, led by David Ellison, also said it plans to nominate its own directors to Warner Bros. Discovery's board. These moves are part of Paramount's attempt to convince shareholders that its $30-per-share all-cash offer is better than Netflix's $27.75-per-share cash-and-stock deal.

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Silver Spikes to Record High as Geopolitical Tensions, Fed Pressures Flare
data.description:
Last year, silver (XAGUSD) posted its best performance since 1979. The precious metal's rally has continued into 2026 as geopolitical tensions and risks to the Fed's independence surge.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Silver notched a new all-time high of $85 on Monday as investors digest President Trump's interest in annexing Greenland and growing unrest in Iran. Silver is viewed as a safe-haven asset and a store of value and tends to perform well in response to geopolitical tensions.

DOJ Investigation into Powell Fuels Silver Prices

Another factor sending silver higher is the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's billion-dollar renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In response, Powell framed the investigation as a politically motivated challenge tied to the Fed's rate-setting policy.

"We see increased interference with the Fed as a key bullish wildcard for the precious metals in 2026," said Carsten Menke, Julius Baer's Head of Next Generation Research.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Don't Fall For FOMO,' Says Top Investor About Micron Stock
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) ended 2025 on a blazing run, one that's continued into the new year. The market seems to agree with CEO Sanjay Mehrotra's description of Micron "as an essential AI enabler," and MU's share price has surged by more than 50% since its Q1 Fiscal 2026 report just a few short weeks ago.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The excitement is understandable, as Micron's Q1 Fiscal 2026 sales of $13.6 billion represented 57% year-over-year and 21% sequential growth. This was its third straight quarter of record revenues, and the company expects more growth up ahead. Indeed, revenue guidance of $18.7 billion (plus or minus $400 million) in Q2 2026 further added to the enthusiasm.

Should investors take this opportunity to jump on the growing momentum? Not according to one investor known by the pseudonym Envision Research, who spots a few worrisome signs.

"I felt it's timely to remind potential investors about a common mistake in investing: the fear of missing out (FOMO)," cautions the 5-star investor.

There are a number of reasons for Envision's concern. One of them relates to insider trading, which Envision notes has been "dominated by selling" during the past three months. The investor even notes that multiple members of the company's C-suite have sold some of their shares.

The company's valuation is also an issue for Envision. The investor explains that Micron operates in a cyclical industry, and therefore argues that price-to-equity ratios can be misleading.

Therefore, even though MU's Forward price-to-earnings ratio of close to 10x looks appealing, Envision cautions that in the past low PE multiples for MU have previously been followed by corrections.

The investor details that Micron's gross margin of 45% during the trailing twelve months is significantly higher than the company's 5-year average. This could start coming down as "incremental industry supply" responds to the elevated pricing of memory chips.

"I see large odds for the current situation to be near a cyclical peak for MU already," adds Envision.

In other words, now isn't the time to jump on the bandwagon, according to the investor.

"Potential investors should control their FOMO and existing investors should start considering hedging strategies," concludes Envision, who gives MU a Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. (To watch Envision Research's track record, click here)

Much of Wall Street begs to differ. With 25 Buys and only 2 Holds, MU cruises to a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $333.58 already reflects plenty of optimism, however, seeing as it represents minimal downside. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Goldman Thinks Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Is Being Underestimated
data.description:
Top Goldman analyst Gabriela Borges initiated coverage on Microsoft (MSFT) with a Buy rating and a $655 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. Borges said investors' views on Microsoft's AI outlook have grown more cautious recently, but she believes those worries are overdone. In her view, Microsoft is better placed than most large tech firms as AI use grows across businesses and consumers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Goldman Is Bullish on Microsoft

The 4-star analyst pointed to Microsoft's reach across the AI stack, from cloud services to AI tools built into everyday products like Office and Windows. That wide presence gives the company more ways to grow as AI adoption picks up.

She also highlighted Microsoft's Copilot tools and AI agents, which are being rolled out across its products. She said this makes AI easier for customers to use and supports steady demand over time.

Because Microsoft controls both the platform and the software, Borges expects the company to deliver more stable growth than rivals that rely on a single product or partner. Looking ahead, Goldman expects that Microsoft could earn more than $35 per share by fiscal 2030 in a stronger scenario. That would mark faster earnings growth than most other large tech companies. 

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 15% rally in its share price over the past year, the average Microsoft price target of $631.36 per share implies 31.73% upside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Tensions between Trump and Fed's Powell
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Monday morning as investors reacted to the news that the Department of Justice (DOJ) subpoenaed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Notably, Powell said that the DOJ has threatened the central bank with a criminal indictment over his testimony this summer about the Fed's building renovations. The move reflects escalating tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and Powell. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.72%, 0.52%, and 0.64%, respectively, at 8:45 a.m. EST on January 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Friday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher, with the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones closing at record highs. Despite a volatile trading week, the indexes ended in positive territory. The S&P 500 gained more than 1%, while the Dow Jones and the Nasdaq Composite rose 2.3% and 1.9%, respectively.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch key economic reports, including the important Consumer Price Index (CPI) data due on Tuesday, Producer Price Index (PPI) data, retail sales, and weekly initial jobless claims.

Moreover, the fourth-quarter earnings season is set to open, with major banks reporting this week. Notably, JPMorgan (JPM), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Morgan Stanley (MS) are scheduled to announce their results this week.

Turning to trending stocks, Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) shares rallied 17% in Monday's pre-market trading on news of its acquisition by Allegiant Travel (ALGT) in a $1.5 billion cash and stock deal. Also, Walmart (WMT) stock gained 3% after the Nasdaq said that the retail giant will be added to the Nasdaq-100 index later this month. Inclusion in this popular index could drive more demand for WMT shares. The Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) tracks the Nasdaq 100. Also, the retailer announced the expansion of drone delivery in partnership with Alphabet-owned Google (GOOGL).

Meanwhile, bank and financial stocks were trending lower after Trump called for a 10% cap on credit card interest rates for one year.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 3 "Strong Buy" Growth Stocks to Buy Now, According to Analysts – 1/12/2026
data.description:
Growth stocks are companies whose earnings and revenue are expected to increase faster than most of their peers. Their strategy to reinvest profits drives continued growth, offering investors both near-term upside and long-term capital appreciation. Thus, investors usually buy them for price gains, not for dividends.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One way to identify these stocks is through their past revenue or earnings growth. Today, we have shortlisted stocks whose revenue has grown at a five-year CAGR of more than 5%. Along with this parameter, we have zeroed in on stocks that have received Strong Buy ratings from Wall Street analysts. 

Here are this week's stocks:

Mastercard (MA) – This global financial services company is focused on digital payments and card-based transaction processing. MA stock's average price target of $684.83 implies an 18.99% upside potential from the current level. The company's revenue has grown at a five-year CAGR of 12.98%.

Importantly, TipRanks AI Analyst expects MA's revenue to grow by 15.67%, compared with the Financial sector's average of 9.73%. Mastercard's revenue growth is driven by strong consumer spending, rising cross‑border travel, and an expanding digital payments and services business.

Arista Networks (ANET) – Arista Networks provides cloud networking solutions, offering high-performance switches and software for data centers, AI workloads, and enterprise environments. ANET stock's average price target of $171.42 implies an upside potential of 39.49%. Its revenue increased at a CAGR of 24.72% in the past five years.

According to TipRanks AI Analyst, ANET's revenue is expected to grow by 27.75% in comparison to the Technology sector's average of 8.5%. The company's revenue growth is aided by strong cloud demand and rising AI‑related networking needs.

Eaton (ETN) – This global power management company provides energy-efficient solutions for electrical, hydraulic, and mechanical systems across industries. ETN stock's average price target of $401.36 implies an upside potential of 23.98%. The company's revenue has grown at a five-year CAGR of 6.85%.

The company's revenue is expected to rise by 8.24%, according to TipRanks AI Analyst. This compares favorably with the Industrials sector's average of 2.89%. Eaton's growth is supported by steady demand for its electrical and power‑management products.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Dow Jones Stalls as Powell Blames DOJ Investigation on Trump Feud
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened lower on Monday after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said on Sunday that the Department of Justice had opened an investigation into him over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters and his testimony of the matter to the Senate Banking Committee last June.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The threat of criminal charges is a consequence of the Federal Reserve setting interest rates based on our best assessment of what will serve the public, rather than following the preferences of the President," said Powell in a statement.

Powell Says Probe Tied to Rate Policy, Not Renovation

Powell said the investigation is not actually about the renovation, but rather about the Fed setting interest rates based on economic conditions instead of President Trump's views. Trump has repeatedly called for lower rates and has previously leveled heavy criticism against Powell for delaying rate cuts. In December, Trump threatened to sue Powell over his "gross incompetence" regarding the renovation.

Trump has denied any involvement with the investigation. "I don't know anything about it, but he's certainly not very good at the Fed, and he's not very good at building buildings," he told NBC.
